title: Step 1: Frontend-only Internet Identity Authentication

requirements.text: Add a login gate to the KD Trust page (KDTrustPage.tsx): when the user is not authenticated via Internet Identity, display a prominent centered prompt with a 'Login with Internet Identity' button using the existing useInternetIdentity hook.

quotes: Add a login gate that shows a full-screen login prompt when the user is not authenticated
Add a login gate that shows Login with Internet Identity when unauthenticated.

acceptanceCriteria: When the user visits the KD Trust page and is not logged in, a login prompt is displayed prominently
The prompt includes a Login with Internet Identity button that triggers the II login flow
After successful login, the user sees the full KD Trust dashboard and AI Hub content

requirements.text: Update the Layout.tsx navigation header to display the authenticated user's principal (truncated, e.g. first 8 and last 4 characters) and a Logout button when the user is logged in via Internet Identity, using the existing useInternetIdentity hook. The logout button should call the logout function from that hook.

quotes: Display the user's principal (truncated) and a logout button in the header.

acceptanceCriteria: When authenticated, the header shows the user's principal truncated
A Logout button is visible in the header when authenticated
Clicking Logout calls the II logout function and returns the user to the unauthenticated state
When not authenticated, neither the principal nor the logout button are shown in the header

requirements.text: Allow the KD Trust page to display the AI Hub and dashboard content in guest mode (unauthenticated), with favorites falling back to localStorage via the existing useKDTrustFavorites hook. Show a non-blocking banner or card prompting login for personalized favorites rather than blocking all content.

quotes: Fall back to localStorage for anonymous users

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users can still browse the AI Hub tools and see the KD Trust dashboard
A non-blocking banner or prompt is shown encouraging users to log in for personalized favorites
Favorites continue to work via localStorage for unauthenticated users
Authenticated users see a visual indicator that their favorites are saved to their identity

constraints: Do not modify backend/main.mo — this step is frontend-only with no backend changes
Do not modify immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
Use the existing useInternetIdentity hook for all authentication state
Preserve all existing KD Trust page functionality including AI Hub filters, tool grid, and KDTrustDashboard
nonGoals: Backend Motoko changes for per-user favorites storage (Step 2)
Wiring frontend favorites to backend API calls (Step 3)
Any changes to KD Ship, NOC, or other pages
